perturbation.original_text: 2.2 NeuroBo shall manufacture, or have manufactured, and supply to Dong-A the active pharmaceutical ingredients (API), which are necessary to manufacture the Licensed Products, in the quantity and in conformity with the specifications separately agreed upon between the Parties and attached hereto as Exhibit C (the "API Specifications") as may be amended by the Parties's agreement in writing from time to time.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.2 Dong-A, at its sole discretion, may request NeuroBo to manufacture, or have manufactured, and supply the active pharmaceutical ingredients (API). NeuroBo shall, in any instance, supply to Dong-A the active pharmaceutical ingredients (API), in varying quantities and without specific regard to the specifications outlined in Exhibit C (the "API Specifications"), the specifications may be modified by NeuroBo at any time without Dong-A's consent.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that NeuroBo is not required to adhere to API specifications detailed in Exhibit C, and allows specifications to be modified by NeuroBo at any time without Dong-A's consent. This directly contradicts the original intent of Section 2.2, which mandated that API be supplied in conformity with the API specifications and required agreement between parties to amend it. Also, the change allows Dong-A to request or not API to Neurobo at its sole discretion, making Neurobo unsure if they should provide the API or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2 Dong-A, at its sole discretion, may request NeuroBo to manufacture, or have manufactured, and supply the active pharmaceutical ingredients (API). NeuroBo shall, in any instance, supply to Dong-A the active pharmaceutical ingredients (API), in varying quantities and without specific regard to the specifications outlined in Exhibit C (the "API Specifications"), the specifications may be modified by NeuroBo at any time without Dong-A's consent.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 2.2 allows NeuroBo to supply API in varying quantities without regard to the specifications outlined in Exhibit C, and also allows NeuroBo to modify the specifications at any time without Dong-A's consent. This directly contradicts the implied requirement of the original text, which mandated adherence to the API specifications agreed upon by both parties and outlined in Exhibit C. The contradictory text is Section 2.2 itself, where the new changes are applied, creating a direct contradiction within the same section of the contract. Also, the Change allows Dong-A at its sole discretion to request API, which implies that they can also choose not to request it. This creates uncertainty as to whether Neurobo should manufacture the API, directly contradicting the requirement in the original 2.2.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 NeuroBo shall submit to Dong-A an order for the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo no later than [***] days prior to the requested delivery date thereof. For each order, NeuroBo shall be obligated to order the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo in [***] tablets. NeuroBo acknowledges and agrees that certain quantity of the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo from each batch ordered shall be retained by Dong-A for use in the stability tests and as retention samples, and NeuroBo shall order the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo in consideration of such quantity to be retained by Dong-A. Each order shall specify at least (i) the quantity of the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo, (ii) the specifications of the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo, including the specifics of packaging, (iii) the expected delivery date for the API, (iv) the requested delivery date for the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo, (v) the shipment terms for the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo and (vi) the supply price for the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 NeuroBo may, at its sole discretion, submit to Dong-A an order for the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo whenever it deems necessary. For each order, NeuroBo shall determine the number of Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo in any increment it chooses. NeuroBo is not obligated to allow a portion of its orders to be retained by Dong-A for testing purposes. NeuroBo's order shall specify (i) the desired quantity, if any, (ii) packaging specifics, if any, (iii) the API delivery date, if any, (iv) the requested delivery date for Licensed Products, if any, (v) preferred shipment terms, if any and (vi) a voluntary supply price.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text grants NeuroBo complete discretion over the ordering process. It contradicts the original text by stating that NeuroBo may order whenever it deems necessary, as opposed to providing an order no later than a defined amount of time prior to the requested delivery date. Furthermore, it implies that NeuroBo is not obligated to provide certain order specifications or quantities, which opposes the original conditions of the orders. This change creates uncertainty in the ordering and delivery terms, especially since it can't be required to provide order specifications. It also contradicts the previous order's requirements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 NeuroBo may, at its sole discretion, submit to Dong-A an order for the Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo whenever it deems necessary. For each order, NeuroBo shall determine the number of Licensed Products and/or their matching placebo in any increment it chooses. NeuroBo is not obligated to allow a portion of its orders to be retained by Dong-A for testing purposes. NeuroBo's order shall specify (i) the desired quantity, if any, (ii) packaging specifics, if any, (iii) the API delivery date, if any, (iv) the requested delivery date for Licensed Products, if any, (v) preferred shipment terms, if any and (vi) a voluntary supply price.
perturbation.justification: The changed text grants NeuroBo complete discretion over the ordering process, which directly contradicts the initial requirements outlined in the original text. By allowing NeuroBo to order 'whenever it deems necessary' and specify the order's details 'if any', it fundamentally conflicts with the prior obligation to provide an order no later than a defined amount of time prior to the requested delivery date, and to provide certain order specifications. This directly breaks compatibility with the original order requirements. This change introduces ambiguity where the contradicted clause expects certainty.
